headline: Onshore slop on a weak short-period pulse — not worth the paddle.
today: SSE wind is on it all day, 15-21mph, and the swell is a mush of short-period SSE. It'll be bumpy and disorganized wherever you look. Wait it out.

daysToWatch.note: Chest-high-plus at a good S angle, best in the morning when the SW cross-on eases to around 10mph. Shortboard or fish, get it before afternoon onshore.
Solid pick — 2.4-3.3ft at 6s with NNE cross-off through morning. Cleanest, most rideable window of the stretch.
Small but prime SE angle with near-glassy dawn. Waist-high log session early before the afternoon SSE onshore fills in.
